text.title: Structured Output Basics — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `structured-output-basics` MUST enforce. Each rule carries an empirical anchor under `source`.

rule.statement: Author the Pydantic model BEFORE writing the prompt. Prompt is generated from / aligned with the schema.
rule.rationale: Schema-second prompts drift; the prompt and schema fall out of sync and the parser fails silently.

rule.statement: Use the provider's structured-output mode (OpenAI response_format=json_schema, Anthropic tool-as-schema) rather than ad-hoc «return JSON» instructions.
rule.rationale: Native modes are server-side-constrained and orders of magnitude more reliable than prompt-only «please return JSON».

rule.statement: Every response is validated through the Pydantic model on receipt. Unvalidated dict access is rejected at review.
rule.rationale: Skipping validation lets soft errors (missing optional → default mismatch) propagate into the DB and corrupt rows.

rule.statement: On ValidationError, retry exactly once with the error message injected into the prompt. Repeated failure escalates to fallback (None + log).
rule.rationale: One-shot retry-with-error catches the long-tail cases where the model hallucinated an extra field; unbounded retries mask underlying schema bugs.

rule.statement: When the downstream consumer is a DB or another agent, set `strict=true` (OpenAI) or use tool-as-schema (Anthropic) for guaranteed conformance.
rule.rationale: Strict mode trades a small latency hit for zero parsing surprises; for high-stakes paths this is the right tradeoff.
